settings.gge9fAeKv0g_text: Why are Korean moisturisers so popular?
settings.ggGcF2ClLc__text: The best Korean moisturiser for each skin type
settings.ggsuc8uMOvV_text:
Whether you’re new to the world of skincare or you’ve spent hours researching, you’ll definitely have heard of Korean moisturisers. But why are they so popular?

 

The secret behind Korean glass skin isn’t magic. It’s moisture. That glossy, bouncy, ultra-smooth complexion is a result of skin being deeply hydrated, balanced, and supported by barrier-friendly ingredients.

 

In this guide, we’ll take a closer look at the power of Korean moisturisers, discover what ingredients make them effective, and explore how to use them for best results.
settings.ggbxIGQz7l3_text: Why are Korean moisturisers so popular?
settings.ggw51K8muVj_text:
Korean skincare has redefined what skincare can be, especially when it comes to hydration. Rather than treating moisturiser as a basic final step, it’s rightfully recognized as a powerful tool that can transform someone's appearance with a smooth, luminous glass skin finish.

 

At the heart of this philosophy is balance. Korean moisturisers are designed to deliver deep, lasting hydration without heaviness or pore-clogging residue. Unlike Western moisturisers, which can sometimes be heavy, Korean blends work to support the skin barrier, lock in moisture, and build long-term resilience in every skin type.

 

This focus on prevention and skin health, not just quick fixes, is what sets Korean skincare apart. The goal isn’t temporary shine, but a hydrated, plump, reflective complexion that looks naturally radiant.
settings.ggQU5aCn5I9_text:
Korean moisturisers are carefully formulated with ingredients that support skin health. This includes:

  • Hyaluronic acid, which pulls water into the skin and keeps it plump and soft.
  • Centella asiatica (Cica), which is the ultimate soothing ingredient. It helps calm redness, reduces irritation, and supports the skin barrier.
  • Snail mucin, which helps repair skin, smooth texture, and lock in hydration without feeling heavy.
  • Ceramides, which keep moisture in, protect against irritants, and make your skin stronger over time.
  • Panthenol (Vitamin B5), which keeps skin hydrated while calming irritation.
  • Botanical extracts like green tea, aloe vera, and licorice root which soothe the skin and reduce inflammation.
settings.ggqar9569_L_text: The best Korean moisturiser for each skin type
settings.ggGZrJGgPQ__text: The best Korean moisturiser for you will depend on your skin type.
settings.ggEb3LFnl-K_text: 1. Beauty of Joseon Red Bean Water Gel
settings.gggil_seT7p_text: For oily skin, lightweight gel moisturisers help hydrate without adding excess shine or clogging pores. Look out for non-comedogenic formulas, oil-regulating ingredients like niacinamide, and lightweight hydration in the form of glycerin or hyaluronic acid. Beauty of Joseon Red Bean Water Gel is a fast-absorbing option that keeps skin fresh and balanced.
settings.ggeAh5XSztk_text: If you have dry skin, you’ll need richer creams that both attract and lock in hydration. Luckily, Korean formulas often combine ingredients like hyaluronic acid and glycerin that pull hydration into the skin. ETUDE SoonJung 2x Barrier Intensive Cream is one of the best Korean face moisturisers for tight or sensitive skin.
settings.ggkokD3tmj0_text: 3. Purito Seoul Hydro Wave Deep Sea Cream
settings.ggRdar2RkDe_text: Finding the right product for combination skin starts with choosing the right texture. Heavy creams can feel suffocating on oil-prone areas, while ultra-light gels may not be enough for drier patches. A lightweight cream or gel-cream that hydrates evenly without leaving residue is usually the best option; luckily, there are plenty of Korean skincare products that fit the bill. Purito Seoul Hydro Wave Deep Sea Cream works well, offering breathable hydration that won’t add shine.
settings.gg98Bxkm-11_text: Final tips to get that glass skin glow
settings.ggusix--Lyp_text:
The way to get glass skin isn’t to layer product on top of product. It’s to find a powerful moisturiser that matches your skin type and deeply hydrates the skin.

 

When your skin is properly moisturized, it looks naturally plump and refined. Fine lines appear softer, rough patches smooth out, and redness is less noticeable. That’s why choosing the right formula matters more than adding extra serums or treatments. A moisturiser that supports your barrier consistently will always do better than a complicated routine.

 

For the best results, focus on being consistent. Apply your moisturiser morning and night, give it time to absorb before layering sunscreen or makeup, and resist the urge to constantly switch products.

 

It also helps to pay attention to seasonal changes. Skin often needs lighter textures in warmer months and more support when temperatures drop. Adjusting your moisturiser as your environment shifts will keep your barrier stable and your glow steady.
